text:
There was an old fox who had never tasted fruit. Every autumn, he’d watch the bright red fruit in the trees. He’d listen, he’d study, he’d fantasize. And every year, he’d forget how to use his paws. One summer, he finally climbed up to a tree and grabbed one. He’d nibble on it, crunching, mumbling some thanks to whatever it was that made it taste so good.

The rest of the animals wondered what he’d found so yummy. Maybe it was a special fern? No, something red. A berry? Nope. A ripe cherry?

Finally, after pondering, he remembered how to eat fruits. He’d spend days tasting everything new.

Sobering up each night, he’d talk to his favorite starfir about his quest to taste every red berry.

“You’ll never finish,” the starfir would say.

“Well,” the fox would think, “what have I got to lose?”

Over seasons, he
